item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's an idea that has been spinning around in my head for a few years now, I've thought about it time and time again, in each related-skip I read. I finally decided that I can't keep denying it even to myself: I hate Prometheus Labs. To me they are the most distasteful, drab and outright cringy GoI there is. I've got nothing against the various authors that published skips made by them, it's just that I don't like them at all, the idea, their concept. If this skip was related to any other group, even non GoI ones, I wouldn't upvote it either(The Foundation using skips is a major no-no for me). But seeing as it's made by PL, it's a firm downvote for me.

I would recommend a rewrite that severed ties with the GoI and made sure to note that the Foundation doesn't use the "organs". But that's just personal taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last remark made me imagine basically a sweatshop where a bunch of former prometheis employees are forced to stamp artificial organs day and night with no means for rest and recreation, all while being whiped by foundation grunts. How close am I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize I'm late, but thank you! In this case, I mostly just used white text because placing normal text right next to normal text would just be painful to look at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Usually, white text (and the related size 0 text) is regarded as redundancy and out-of-universe element. After all, if the author whites it out, why should a reader care about it? Especially when there is no indication that a reader should care about white text.

But here, you gave an in-universe justification of the phenomenon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think I can go with the "Sole Survivor" idea, but I made an attempt to fix the other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat, I like how you came to frame the final element; it solved my issues about the PL researchers having access to the SCP file itself.

Content-wise though, I'm not feeling that portion. I would prefer something of substance in the file, with the note sprinkled in it, rather than the entire 'file' being said note.

It's also a little weak, because there's a bit of a tonal shift between it and the 'p.s.'; it begins with an almost spoopy level of threatening, and the 'p.s.' comes off as lighthearted and almost comedic.

Finally, as it was a late addition, O think it could use just a touch more foreshadowing in the interview. Novote for the time being.

As an aside, I still think it would have been cool for L themselves to have been the sleeper agent - had the recovery unfolded differently, and they were the "sole survivor" of the CI attack.

item.title: Re: Congrats Man!
item.content:encoded: Thanks… please upvote it. The sheer joy I'm feeling even with a +7 is amazing.

item.title: Congrats Man!
item.content:encoded: Still haven't looked at it thoroughly enough to decide how I may vote on it, but it's leaning towards a +1 atm. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed how thoroughly thought out this was. You clearly did your homework. I still feel like it would read better if you consolidated all the sub-elements under the SCP-2687 banner and mentioning them as aspects of that anomaly rather than the separate portions of the process being described separately. I can see why you did it because of the disparate elements of the article but ultimately it didn't reduce my enjoyment of the piece. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
After a lot of critique, I present to you SCP-2687, my first SCP.

Thanks to LudwigThePerplexing, Dann2442, tretter, Roget, and the several others that looked over this.

If you happened to critique this and I, for whatever reason, forgot about you, please PM me. I'll add you to the list.

Special thanks to Roget for giving my permission to add this to the Site-77 hub and giving me some recommendations for what SCPs to use in the first step of the organ creation process.

A small explanation
It's really small

After essentially being forced to work for them, Dr. Lienau sees the Foundation the same way the Serpent's Hand sees the GOC: A bunch of idiots that only get in the way.
